overview: A single Go backend service exposes a minimal REST API for managing todos. The service persists data to a local file-backed JSON store with mutex-guarded concurrency, and communicates via HTTP/JSON on port 9090.
requirements: Provide REST endpoints to list, create, and delete todos
Each todo has id, title, completed (default false), createdAt
IDs are unique and never reused
GET /todos returns 200 with array ordered by creation time (empty array when none)
POST /todos validates non-empty title, returns 201 with created todo, 400 on invalid
DELETE /todos/{id} returns 204 on success, 404 if not found
Return 400 for malformed JSON, 405 for unsupported methods, 500 for internal errors
All responses are JSON UTF-8 with proper Content-Type
Handle 50+ concurrent requests safely without duplicate IDs

components.name: todo-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Minimal REST API for managing todos.
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status: { type: string }
  /todos:
    get:
      summary: List todos
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Array of todos
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
    post:
      summary: Create todo
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [title]
              properties:
                title: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
        '400':
          description: Bad request
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
  /todos/{id}:
    delete:
      summary: Delete todo
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: id
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      responses:
        '204': { description: Deleted }
        '404':
          description: Not found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
components:
  schemas:
    Todo:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: { type: string }
        title: { type: string }
        completed: { type: boolean }
        createdAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
    Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        message: { type: string }
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a Go service using net/http on port 9090.

Routes:
- GET /health -> 200 {"status":"ok"}
- GET /todos -> 200 JSON array of all todos sorted ascending by createdAt.
- POST /todos -> parse JSON {title}. If body malformed -> 400 {"message":"invalid JSON"}. If title missing/empty/whitespace -> 400 {"message":"title is required"}. Else create todo with UUID id (crypto/rand-based or google/uuid), completed=false, createdAt=time.Now().UTC(). Persist and return 201 with created todo.
- DELETE /todos/{id} -> if exists remove and return 204 (no body). Else 404 {"message":"todo not found"}.
- Any other method on these paths -> 405 {"message":"method not allowed"} with Allow header.
- Unknown paths -> 404.

Persistence: JSON file at path from env TODO_DATA_FILE (default /data/todos.json). On startup, create directory if missing and load existing data (empty list if file absent). On every mutation, write atomically (write to temp file then os.Rename). Guard all access with a sync.RWMutex. IDs are never reused (UUIDs guarantee this).

All responses set Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8. Panics recovered by middleware returning 500 {"message":"internal server error"} and logging stack. Log every request: method, path, status, duration.

Use only standard library plus optionally github.com/google/uuid. Provide Dockerfile (multi-stage build, distroless or alpine) exposing 9090 and declaring VOLUME /data. Include go.mod, main.go and handler/store files as appropriate.
